changes_summary: Addressed three significant flags: (1) FLAG-001 — Added `isPeerClearingRef` guard to prevent boomerang clearing; the peer-clear callback now sets a ref flag before clearing local state, and the sync effect skips the gallery clear when this flag is set. Also added `deselectGalleryItems(currentIds)` in the peer-clear callback to remove bridge-owned items from the gallery map. (2) FLAG-002 — Documented that toggle/append in the gallery does not fire peer-clear (matching existing video-editor behavior) and added the `deselectGalleryItems` call in peer-clear to clean up bridge items when non-toggle selection happens. Reframed as a known design decision, not a bug. (3) FLAG-003 — Changed the batch editor bridge input from `selection.selectedIds` to `isMobile ? selection.mobileSelectedIds : selection.selectedIds` so mobile batch selections are also bridged to GallerySelectionContext. Added a boomerang-specific smoke test step.
questions: The batch editor's `useSelection` hook stores mobile selections in `mobileSelectedIds` and desktop in `selectedIds`. The bridge reads `isMobile ? mobileSelectedIds : selectedIds`. Is there ever a case where both arrays have content simultaneously (e.g., switching from desktop to mobile mid-session), and should the bridge merge them?

success_criteria.criterion: Selecting images in the video travel timeline causes them to appear as attachments in the global AgentChat component
Selecting images in the video travel batch editor causes them to appear as attachments in the global AgentChat component
Selecting images in the image generation gallery continues to show them as attachments in AgentChat (no regression)
Deselecting all images in any surface removes the attachments from AgentChat
Non-toggle selection in one surface clears selection in other surfaces (mutual exclusivity via peer-clear)
Peer-clear does not boomerang: when gallery selects and fires peer-clear, the bridge clears local state without calling clearGallerySelection
Peer-clear removes bridge-owned items from gallery map via deselectGalleryItems so subsequent toggle/append doesn't merge stale items
Mobile batch editor selections (mobileSelectedIds) are bridged to GallerySelectionContext
Sending a message in AgentChat clears the gallery selection and thus the local surface selection
The bridge hook is reusable and under ~60 lines — no over-abstraction
No changes to existing selection UX behavior in any surface (click, toggle, range-select, drag-to-select all continue working)
Bridge hook unmount cleanup: clears gallery selection and unregisters peer-clear
Manual smoke test across all three surfaces confirms end-to-end flow including boomerang test

assumptions: The image generation gallery (GenerationsPaneGallery) is already fully wired to GallerySelectionContext and needs no changes — confirmed by code reading.
The video travel timeline and batch editor are never both actively interactive at the same time (they're different modes of the same ShotEditor), so sharing the single registerPeerClear slot is safe.
GenerationRow fields (imageUrl, location, type, contentType, generation_id, primary_variant_id) provide sufficient data to build GallerySelectionMeta without additional queries.
The bridge hook should use useGallerySelectionOptional() (not the throwing version) so it degrades gracefully if context is ever absent.
Unmounting the travel-between-images page should clear any gallery selection that was set by its bridge, so AgentChat doesn't show stale attachments.
Toggle/append gallery operations merging with bridge items (when user Cmd+clicks without first doing a non-modifier click) is acceptable behavior matching the existing video-editor pattern.
structure_warnings: Each step section should reference at least one file in backticks.
